issues.detail: The expected row still attributes to "its task." Residual single-tree wording also remains in AT-034.02 ("task association"/"real task"), AT-034.03 ("without a task"), AT-034.10 ("task/bucket attribution"), and AT-034.16 ("task's scope").
issues.fix: Consistently name the attribution target as the pulled PM-tree requirement; retain "taskless" only for exploration/onboarding buckets.

issues.detail: The dishonest-binding fixture is merely "a real task of this project." That could be a dev-tree item or an unpulled PM item, neither of which is a resolvable attribution target under d82.
issues.fix: Define the spoofed target as a real, currently pulled PM-tree requirement belonging to the project; require no PM-requirement association for unresolved fixtures.

issues.detail: The unresolved-binding matrix omits the two-tree boundary: a same-project dev-tree item, including one related to a pulled PM requirement, and a same-project PM requirement lacking a valid active pull.
issues.fix: Add both fixtures and assert served plus exactly unattributed, no implicit dev-to-PM roll-up, no dev association stored, and a binding-failure signal.

issues.detail: No test proves that pull authority, rather than an apparent In Progress status, makes a PM binding valid, or that vendor-native dev merge-to-Done leaves attribution bound to the PM requirement until verified PM completion.
issues.fix: Test an unauthorized PM status mutation, a dev-tree merge-to-Done, verified PM completion, and reuse of the stale completed binding; only the valid pull interval should attribute to the PM requirement, while every request remains ungated.

issues.detail: "No finer-than-requirement detail EXISTS ... anywhere" is broader than the requirement, which mandates surface/actor metadata in each usage row. d82 forbids finer attribution targets, not all finer operational metadata.
issues.fix: Scope the assertion to attribution associations and NGO-displayed aggregation: no dev-item attribution field or association and no finer-than-requirement burn line, while required actor/surface metadata remains permitted.

issues.detail: The no-dev-item-field probe does not exercise a populated dev tree, so a generic task relation, implicit parent roll-up, baseline join, or NGO-view join could leak dev-tree identifiers, titles, status, or burn while still passing.
issues.fix: Seed uniquely identifiable dev items and vendor-native status/merge events; prove they create no attribution target, baseline dimension, or NGO burn line and that only their explicitly pulled PM requirement may receive burn.

issues.detail: The PRD's Story 5, REQ-008, the trailing REQ-028 conventions/acceptance criterion, and Technical Considerations still describe the old single-tree authorities: self-assignment to In Progress, merge-to-Done for the NGO-facing tree, generic task-ID binding, and blanket status reversion.
issues.fix: Retarget those sections to d82: PM In Progress only from explicit pull, PM Done only from verified completion, attribution only to pulled PM requirements, and dev-tree vendor-native status/merge behavior is NGO-hidden and revert-exempt.

issues.detail: REQ-036 still says kickoff creates exactly one bootstrap "task" and authoring is attributed to that task, while REQ-026 now seeds PM requirements plus a bootstrap item and REQ-034 permits attribution only to pulled PM-tree requirements. The bootstrap item's tree/type and eligibility are unresolved.
issues.fix: Define the bootstrap as an explicit PM-tree requirement eligible for pull and requirement-level attribution, or assign it a specified taskless category; reconcile the kickoff cardinality language and add an AT-034 bootstrap-attribution fixture.
